intro:desc:
Solo role-playing game, Vagabondage lets you play as a protagonist on the run in the French countryside. Between survival and encounters, it uses the web portal of maps and aerial photographs of the IGN.
more help...
about:desc:
Vagabondage is a solo role-playing game played online on IGN's maps and aerial photographs.
Between survival and encounters, you play a character on the run in the French countryside.
about:accroche: Your only chance to survive: know how to read a map!
about:ignLicense:
The game uses SCAN 25® data (maps and associated online services) distributed under license terms available on the IGN website, for which IGN holds the intellectual property rights. The use of this Scan Data and/or IGN Geoservices is strictly limited to the player’s Private Use. Reproduction for distribution to third parties or commercialization of graphic outputs is prohibited, in any form or by any means.
© IGN – 2022 – More info...
help:clickDrag: move the point along a road
help:mapAccess: access the map (know where to go / find points of interest)
help:step1:
You are here!
Click on the red circle and drag the cursor to where you want to go, the route appears on the terrain, then release the button.
help:step2: To cut across fields to another road, do a "long click" on the red circle before moving, or hold the <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> key.
help:step3:
Walking will exhaust you.
Your survival depends on what you find to drink and eat along the way.
help:step4: At each step, click the die and look around you to find something to drink or make an encounter.
help:step5: What you find along the way will be stored in your backpack.
help:step6: To find your position or navigate the terrain, use the map.
gauge:hydroHelp: You need to drink something to recover a hydration point.
gauge:foodHelp: You need to eat something to recover a sustenance point.
gauge:restHelp: You need to rest to recover a fatigue point.
gauge:inanHelp:
You urgently need to rest or eat something.
Drinking or eating something will earn you a fatigue point back. Otherwise, you will lose an additional point every hour if you do nothing.
road:good: Keep to the small roads and everything will be fine...
road:bad: Try to take paths and small country roads to escape your pursuers.
road:info: The more you use busy roads, the more likely you are to have a bad encounter.
legend:help2: Look for water points on the map...
legend:help3: ...and other information to find water, food or rest...

place:croix:title: A cross by the roadside.

place:croix:0:1:desc: A hiker passes by. He offers you a drink.

An old woman is carrying full baskets. She trips and you help her up.
She offers you an apple.
